title: Werauhia vanhyningii
taxonomy: Werauhia vanhyningii is classified within the family Bromeliaceae. This family includes a wide variety of tropical plants commonly known as air plants or pineapples. The genus Werauhia itself is a group of terrestrial bromeliads native to Central and South America. Bromeliads are further grouped into several subfamilies, and Werauhia vanhyningii's classification is within the subfamily Tillandsioideae. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the family.

Its leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, making it useful for treating wounds and infections. intro: Werauhia vanhyningii, also known as Van Hyning's bromeliad, is a species of bromeliad plant native to the tropical forests of Costa Rica. The health benefits of Werauhia vanhyningii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable addition to traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
